title: gynoxys seleriana
intro: Gynoxys seleriana, also known as Selerianth or Gold of the Andes, is a plant species native to the Andean region, characterized by its golden-yellow flowers and medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gynoxys seleriana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
